title: Duel on Kuar
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}}
{{Conjecture|legends}}

{{Quote|I'm offering you a chance to deal with me simply, with one stroke of your vaunted lightsaber. Meet me on my own world…on the plains of Harkul, where you and I will battle to the death.|Mandalore the Indomitable, to Ulic Qel-Droma|<ref name="TSW1" />}}
In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3996 BBY]], a '''duel''' took place on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kuar]] between the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] and [[Mandalore the Indomitable]], [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|leader]] of the [[Mandalorian Crusaders]]. In the months following the alliance between fellow [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords of the Sith]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] and Qel-Droma, the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] clans attempted to begin a [[Krath Holy Crusade|new crusade]] under their [[Warlord/Legends|warlord's]] banner, believing that they would be able to gain control over the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]] while the attention of Qel-Droma and his [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] [[Sith cult/Legends|cult]] was focused elsewhere.

Engaging in several small raids to test the strength of the Krath, Mandalore eventually caught the attention of Qel-Droma by destroying an important [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] [[Tetan carbonite smelter|smelting station]] in the Empress Teta [[Star system/Legends|system]]. Wanting to crush the threat that the Mandalorians presented as soon as possible, Qel-Droma agreed to a challenge of single combat on the planet Kuar extended by Mandalore. The duel, as explained by Mandalore, would be fought between Qel-Droma and himself, and the victor would receive full command over their foes' forces, as well as the seven worlds of Empress Teta. Qel-Droma managed to best Mandalore during the course of the fight, but refused to [[Death/Legends|kill]] his defeated enemy. Having experienced the fighting ability of Mandalore firsthand, Qel-Droma believed that Mandalore was of more use to him alive. Seeing that his reputation had been preserved, Mandalore pledged his life, honor, and [[Troop/Legends|troops]] to Qel-Droma's cause.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|He wants the seven worlds of Teta. He wants to conquer. I believe he feels a burning need even he doesn't understand.|Aleema Keto, speaking to Ulic Qel-Droma of Mandalore the Indomitable|<ref name="TSW1" />}}
[[File:UlicVsMandalore.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The combatants face each other.]]
In [[3996 BBY]], the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after the [[Battle of Empress Teta|Second Battle of Empress Teta]],<ref name="NEC" /> in which [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] and [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] were anointed [[Sith Lord/Legends|Lords of the Sith]], the two [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] made a plan through which they would expand their forces in an effort to eventually topple the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and destroy the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Part of the plan was for Qel-Droma to use the [[Army/Legends|armies]] of his [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] [[Sith cult/Legends|cult]] to begin asserting his control in the [[Star system/Legends|systems]] neighboring [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta]]. With the Krath military attempting to fulfill their commander's orders, the seven [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] of [[Empress Teta/Legends|Empress Teta]] appeared to be overstretched by their campaigns. This, however, did not cause Qel-Droma or his co-[[Commander/Legends|commander]], [[Aleema Keto]], any concern, for they believed that no enemy other than perhaps a joint Republic–[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] force could threaten them. Nonetheless, their overconfidence in their own power allowed for another enemy to attack them—the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]].<ref name="TSW1" />

The Mandalorians were a warrior society whose beliefs were firmly ingrained with their sense of honor. Attempting to gain both territory and honor by attacking the Tetan worlds, the [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalorian leader]], [[Mandalore the Indomitable]], led his [[Mandalorian Crusaders|soldiers]] on several raids within the boundaries of Krath [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. Eventually, following the destruction of an important [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] [[Tetan carbonite smelter|smelting station]] under Krath control, these raids managed to attract the attention of Ulic Qel-Droma. Believing that he did not have time to deal with [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] such as the Mandalorians, Qel-Droma contacted Mandalore and demanded that he and his armies surrender immediately. Mandalore, however, refused Qel-Droma's demands, choosing instead to challenge Qel-Droma to a one-on-one fight that would decide the fate of the seven worlds of Empress Teta. Qel-Droma agreed to Mandalore's demand for a fight, but also offered up his own terms should he win the fight—if he could defeat Mandalore, then the Mandalorian clans would pledge their allegiance to him, and thus fight under his war standards. Not wanting his honor to be tarnished, Mandalore agreed to Qel-Droma's terms.<ref name="TSW1" />

==The duel==
{{Quote|Your defeat is at hand, Jedi!|Mandalore the Indomitable|<ref name="TSW1" />}}
[[File:MandalorevsUlic.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Ulic Qel-Droma versus Mandalore the Indomitable]]
As part of Mandalore's terms, both he and Qel-Droma, along with their respective armies, were to meet on [[Kuar]], on the [[Plains of Harkul]],<ref name="TSW1" /> a [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] world which the Mandalorians had raided and established camps upon years earlier.<ref name="Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> Believing that he could stunt Qel-Droma's use of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], Mandalore waited until just before the fight to advise the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Lord that the duel would not take place on the ground; instead, it would take place far above the plains of Harkul on a network of linked chains. Likewise, Mandalore advised those listening that to even the odds against a Jedi, he would be allowed to use his customized [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droid]], while Qel-Droma would not be allowed any type of mount.<ref name="TSW1" />

The duel began with Mandalore attacking Qel-Droma from above, using the advantage afforded to him by his Basilisk mount. Qel-Droma, however, was able to use his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and [[Force power/Legends|Force abilities]] to destroy Mandalore's mount, thus forcing the Mandalorian [[Chief/Legends|chieftain]] to fight hand-to-hand. Realizing that Qel-Droma still held an advantage over him by being allowed the use of his lightsaber, Mandalore challenged Qel-Droma to put away his lightsaber and fight with a [[Mythosaur axe/Legends|Mythosaur axe]], the traditional bladed [[Weapon/Legends|weapon]] of the Mandalorian forefathers. Believing that he could defeat Mandalore regardless of the weapon he used, Qel-Droma accepted the Mandalorian weapon. The fight continued until, ultimately, Qel-Droma was able to knock Mandalore off of the chain linking, thus defeating the Mandalorian leader.<ref name="TSW1" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|I am not interested in your death, Mandalore. I have a better fate in mind for you. A much better fate.|Ulic Qel-Droma, to Mandalore|<ref name="TSW1" />}}
[[File:MandaloreTheDominated.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The defeat of Mandalore the Indomitable]]
Realizing that he was defeated, Mandalore asked Qel-Droma to [[Death/Legends|kill]] him, thus putting a proper end to the fight. Qel-Droma, however, had a different plan for the defeated chieftain; realizing that he would need a trustworthy and combat-capable [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] to lead his newly acquired Mandalorian [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], Qel-Droma advised Mandalore that he would act as his personal warlord. Thus, with the Mandalorian armies under his command, Qel-Droma began preparations to further his and Kun's plans for [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] domination, deploying his new allies to [[Battle of Foerost (Great Sith War)|seize]] Republic [[Warship/Legends|warships]] from the [[Foerost Shipyards|shipyards]] in [[Foerost system/Legends|orbit]] over the uninhabited planet [[Foerost/Legends|Foerost]].<ref name="TSW1" />

While Mandalore had accepted his defeat at the hands of the Krath warlord Qel-Droma, several of his supporters had not. One such individual was a young Mandalorian of the [[Zeltron/Legends|Zeltron]]<ref name="KOTOR48">[[Knights of the Old Republic 48|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 48]]</ref> [[Species/Legends|species]]<ref name="Pleasure Planet">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040105zeltros|text=Zeltros: Pleasure Planet|int=Zeltros: Pleasure Planet}}</ref> named [[Demagol|Antos Wyrick]], who was devastated that his leader, from whom he had learned martial combat directly, had been defeated. Following Mandalore's loss, Wyrick sneaked into Qel-Droma's cabin with intentions of determining what gave the Jedi a tactical advantage over the leader of the vaunted Mandalorian clans. All Wyrick found was a worn [[Arca Jeth's robe|Jedi robe]], which he confiscated for later study with hopes of discovering a genetic secret to Qel-Droma's superiority over Mandalore. Unbeknownst to Wyrick at the time, the stolen [[Jedi apparel/Legends|garment]] had not been worn by Qel-Droma; it had belonged to the Sith Lord's deceased [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], the [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] Jedi [[Arca Jeth]].<ref name="KOTOR48" />

==Behind the scenes==
The duel on Kuar first appeared in the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|initial issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War]]'' series of [[Star Wars comic books|comics]], written by author [[Kevin J. Anderson]], drawn by artist [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]],<ref name="TSW1" /> and published on [[August 15]]th, [[1995]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54595/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_the_sith_war_1995_1|text=Star Wars: Tales Of The Jedi - The Sith War (1995) #1}}</ref>
